commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: Wheat futures $5.96/bu (Apr 16). USDA season-average raised to $5.00/bu (+5c in April WASDE). Crop insurance spring price $6.19/bu. Fertilizer crisis ($826/MT urea, +35%) remains long-term driver.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: July corn $4.56/bu (Apr 16). Pre-war $4.31. Fertilizer costs driving up planting costs — USDA: farmers planting 3% less corn, 4% more soybeans. Urea $826/ton.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: July soybeans $11.82/bu (Apr 16). USDA: 84.7M acres planned (+4%) as farmers shift from corn. Season-average $10.30/bu (+10c).

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: Sugar #11 (ICE) at $0.224/lb (April 2). Prices reversing earlier decline as shipping costs and diesel surcharges ripple through global supply chains. Hormuz closure driving up transport costs even for non-Gulf commodities. Sugar now modestly above pre-war levels after lagging other commodities.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica ~$2.90/lb (Apr 16). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41. Surplus of ~10M bags expected. Container rates still up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.note: USDA projects 3.6% overall food price increase in 2026 (food-at-home +2.5%, food-away-from-home +3.7%). Sugar/sweets +6.7%. Wolfe Research estimates war could add ~2 additional percentage points. Fertilizer crisis (urea +40%, ammonia +41%) threatening Northern Hemisphere planting season.

fertilizer.detail: Urea surged to $826/MT (+35% jump, +74% from pre-war $475). Ammonia topped $1,000/ton for first time since 2023 ($990-$1,250 range). Hormuz carries ~50% of world's urea exports and ~30% of ammonia. USDA: farmers planting 3% less corn, 4% more soybeans due to fertilizer costs. Corn-urea ratio at 5-year high (87-90 bu/ton).

context: Day 48 (Ceasefire Day 9): Wheat $5.96 (+6.0%), corn $4.56 (+5.8%), soybeans $11.82 (+16.8%), coffee ~$2.90. FAO Food Price Index 128.5 (+2.4% in March). FERTILIZER CRISIS DEEPENING: urea $826/ton (+74% from pre-war, +35% jump), ammonia topped $1,000/ton. USDA: 3% less corn, 4% more soy planted. EIA: Q2 peak $115/bbl. IMF: 3.1% growth, recession warning. Oil Brent $94.66. Gas $4.108. Average weekly grocery bill $181 (+7.7%).
